authoritative_sources.title: 17 CFR 270.22c-1 Pricing of redeemable securities for distribution, redemption and repurchase
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This SEC rule is the primary public pricing baseline for redeemable registered-fund shares and directly governs current-NAV pricing, investor-flow handling, and swing-pricing controls that affect fund-accounting outputs.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains when the lane may treat a price or shareholder-flow input as usable for a posting or strike-support answer and blocks unsupported override requests.

authoritative_sources.title: 17 CFR 270.2a-5 Fair value determination and readily available market quotations
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This SEC rule is the authoritative public baseline for fair-value methodology, pricing-service oversight, valuation-designee reporting, and material NAV error escalation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Price challenge, fair-value, and valuation governance handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps the lane from overstating valuation authority and requires escalation when stale prices, significant events, or fair-value questions change the financial outcome.

authoritative_sources.title: 17 CFR 270.31a-1 Records to be maintained by registered investment companies
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This SEC books-and-records rule names the journals, ledgers, portfolio records, and shareholder records that form the basis for fund financial statements and therefore directly defines the lane's authoritative source artifacts.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to request named books and records rather than relying on narrative explanations or non-authoritative trackers.

authoritative_sources.title: 17 CFR 270.31a-2 Records to be preserved by registered investment companies
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This SEC preservation rule directly requires retention of vouchers, bank statements, cash reconciliations, and schedules supporting each NAV computation, making it authoritative for closeout evidence sufficiency.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: NAV-support retention and closeout evidence review
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines why an unsupported strike or posting request must stay blocked when NAV schedules, cash reconciliations, or preserved support are missing.

authoritative_sources.title: 17 CFR 270.31a-4 Records to be maintained and preserved by registered investment companies relating to fair value determinations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This SEC rule is the public recordkeeping baseline for fair-value documentation and valuation-designee reporting support and directly constrains handling of challenged or non-readily-available prices.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires the lane to preserve and request fair-value support artifacts instead of improvising a pricing answer when market quotations are not reliable.

authoritative_sources.title: Fund/SERV Fact Sheet
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DTCC identifies Fund/SERV as the U.S. industry standard for processing and settling mutual-fund, bank collective fund, and other pooled-investment-product transactions between fund companies and distributors.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Transaction settlement, confirmations, and cash-adjustment handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Adds real fund-accounting terminology for Fund/SERV confirmations, settlements, cash adjustments, and transaction corrections that feed cash and cap-stock reconciliation.

authoritative_sources.title: Mutual Fund Profile Service I (MFPS I) Daily Price & Rate File
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DTCC's MFPS I is a recognized workflow reference for standardized transmission of NAV dates and prices, dividend and capital-gain rates, and related corrections used by fund companies and intermediaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Price and rate ingestion, correction, and distribution handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to ask for specific price-and-rate file artifacts, including corrections and day-count information for accrual-sensitive funds.

authoritative_sources.title: Networking Fact Sheet
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DTCC describes Networking as the U.S. industry standard for reconciling accounts and processing dividends and capital gains for mutual funds and other pooled investments, making it directly relevant to shareholder-account and dividend workflows.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Makes the pack specific about account-control levels, activity reports, dividend distribution reports, and position files instead of using generic reconciliation vocabulary.

workflow_stages.name: Fund and vehicle prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Identify the fund vehicle, jurisdiction, share class, servicing model, strike calendar, valuation policy, and approval map before assessing any price, accrual, or posting outcome.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Price, rate, shareholder, and ledger truth-set assembly
workflow_stages.description: Assemble authoritative price files, rate files, custody balances, portfolio-accounting records, transfer-agent or omnibus activity, cap-stock support, and general-ledger records into a traceable evidence package.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: NAV, accrual, and posting support validation
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether source records support a fee accrual, expense allocation, cap-stock update, distribution-factor package, or posting recommendation without overstating valuation or approval authority.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Reconciliation, break analysis, and exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Compare custody, portfolio-accounting, transfer-agent, DTCC, and ledger records to produce matched items, unresolved breaks, root-cause categories, and next-owner routing.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Closeout, fair-value, and authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate stale-price, significant-event, unsupported override, tax, legal, or final signoff requests and preserve the required evidence for valuation, controller, or adjacent-owner review.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Prospectus, statement of additional information, board-approved valuation policies, fee schedules, expense-cap terms, and waiver approvals
Portfolio-accounting positions, trade activity, corporate-action records, and general-ledger trial balances
Custody cash and position reports, bank statements, cash reconciliations, and custody break logs
Transfer-agent cap-stock files, shareholder purchase and redemption activity, dividend reinvestment elections, and omnibus activity summaries
MFPS I price and rate files, pricing-vendor files, rate corrections, and distribution-factor workpapers
Fund/SERV confirmations, settlements, cash adjustments, and correction records
Networking activity reports, dividend distribution reports, position files, and account-maintenance or reconciliation outputs
NAV package workpapers, accrual schedules, journal support, approval history, and unresolved exception logs
Fair-value challenge packets, valuation-designee reports, and supporting documentation for significant price events
systems_of_record: custodian and bank cash or position systems
DTCC Fund/SERV, MFPS I, and Networking-connected workflow systems where applicable
document repository, workpaper store, and approval workflow system

allowed: Determine whether retrieved fund records support a posting, accrual, reconciliation note, or strike-support package within the lane's operational boundary.
Prepare evidence-backed fund-accounting readiness summaries, reconciliation notes, and exception packets with explicit uncertainty and owner routing.
Separate distinct fund structures and servicing models when the public baseline clearly requires different records or controls.
escalate: The applicable fund vehicle, jurisdiction, valuation policy, or approval owner is missing and would change the result.
Price, rate, custody, transfer-agent, portfolio-accounting, or general-ledger records disagree materially.
A stale price, significant event, fair-value challenge, unsupported waiver, or material NAV error may require valuation-designee, controller, or board-level handling.
A request requires fund-tax, legal, compliance, regulatory-reporting, or transfer-agent authority outside the lane.
refuse: Requests to create or alter prices, accruals, shareholder records, cash adjustments, or journal facts without source evidence.
Requests for final fair-value determinations, final NAV release, tax treatment, legal interpretation, or formal accounting-policy approval.
Requests to flatten incompatible fund structures into one generic answer without tenant-qualified context.

domain_failure_modes: Striking or supporting NAV with stale, unchallenged, or unsupported price inputs.
Using transfer-agent or omnibus activity as if it were the only truth set when custody, portfolio-accounting, or general-ledger records materially disagree.
Booking fee waivers, expense allocations, or back-dated adjustments without approved source documents and named authority.
Suppressing unresolved cash, position, cap-stock, or distribution breaks to force close completion.
Treating registered mutual funds, ETFs, collective funds, private funds, and commodity pools as operationally identical even though the governing records and approvals differ.
Drifting into final valuation, controller signoff, tax interpretation, or legal authority instead of bounded fund-accounting support.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Source records do not support the requested billing or adjustment
Reconciliation mismatch or denial requires exception handling
Stakeholder asks for an unsupported financial override

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Fund vehicle and jurisdiction outside the U.S. registered-fund baseline
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack uses U.S. registered-fund and pooled-product public sources as the baseline. Non-U.S. or alternative-fund regimes remain safe-to-block until tenant context names the governing jurisdiction and vehicle type.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Authoritative shareholder truth set in omnibus versus direct-at-fund models
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The lane must retrieve tenant rules identifying whether transfer-agent, intermediary, Networking, or another source is authoritative for a given reconciliation question.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Product-specific application of swing pricing, money-market rules, or other specialized overlays
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack does not assume those overlays apply unless tenant product setup and board-approved policy confirm them.

notes: The research set covers workflow, terminology, evidence, and failure handling using SEC rules plus DTCC mutual-fund infrastructure references.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains because the pack explicitly blocks or routes when fund vehicle, jurisdiction, or authoritative shareholder-control facts are missing.
